Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF YES ONLINE, INC'S Motion For Assignment is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Off Calendar

Notice Of Prove Up Hearing For Default Judgment And Application For Attorneys Fees And Costs Ursuant To Ccp 585(D)
Off Calendar Nov-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.
Sean W. McBride, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Marcella Fredrickson.

No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Bananaberry Sitters LLC, a California Limited Liability Company, and Sherissa Berry.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
The court having continued matter this day for counsel to submit additional declarations and Points and Authorities in reference to the issue of reasonable attorneys' fees and Pre-judgment interest calculation based on the amounts held a further hearing.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:
I. Defendantants shall, within thirty days of the service of this Judgment, establish a qualified escrow fund and place into said fund $97,510.44 for improper withdrawals for the period of December 2009 through and including October 2011; and
II. Defendants shall, within Thirty days of this Judgment, Pay to Plaintiff $54,327.31 out of the Escrow Fund, representing the following amounts:
A. 20% of the improper withdrawals, totaling $19,502.09, in delayed compensation for Plaintiff's services to Defendants; plus
B. $20,000.00 that sum representing a reasonable fair market value of Plaintiff's dissenting membership interest in the Company; and
C. Plaintiff's reasonable attorneys' fees and costs in the amount of $8,385.70, pursuant to CCP 1021.5; and
D. Pre-judgment interest at the rate of ten percent (10%) per annum on the total judgment which consists of item I. from the date of the filing of tis action until fully paid, in the total amount of $6,439.52.
III. Defendants shall, within thirty days of the service of this Judgment, cause the winding up and dissolution of Defendant Bananaberry Sitters LLC, upon the satisfaction of which the remaining funds in the Escrow Fund shall be returned to Sherissa Berry.
IV. In the event Defendants fail or refuse to bring about the dissolution of the Defendant Company, this Court will entertain such proceedings as may be necessary for the proper dissolution of the Company.
V. This Court shall retain jurisdiction in this matter.

Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Prove Up Hearing For Default Judgment And Application For Attorneys Fees And Costs Ursuant To Ccp 585(D)
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Continued To Nov-05-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Sean W. McBride, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Marcella Fredrickson.

No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Bananaberry Sitters LLC, a California Limited Liability Company, and Sherissa Berry.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

Marcella Fredrickson sworn and examinded on behalf of Plaintiff.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

I. Defendantants shall, within thirty days of the service of this Judgment, establish a qualified escrow fund and place into said fund $97,510.44 for improper withdrawals for the period of December 2009 through and including October 2011; and
II. Defendants shall, within fifteen days of this Judgment, Pay to Plaintiff $53,829.40 out of the Escrow Fund, representing the following amounts:
A. 20% of the improper withdrawals, totaling $19,502.09, in delayed compensation for Plaintiff's services to Defendants; plus
B. $20,000.00 that sum representing a reasonable fair market value of Plaintiff's dissenting membership interest in the Company; and
C. The court reserves the issued of reasonable attorneys' fees pending the file of Points and authorities by plaintiff's counsel.
D. Pre-judgment interest calculation to be submitted to the court based on the amounts granted by the court.
III. Defendants shall, within thirty days of the service of this Judgment, cause the winding up and dissolution of Defendant Bananaberry Sitters LLC, upon the satisfaction of which the remaining funds in the Escrow Fund shall be returned to Sherissa Berry.
IV. In the event Defendants fail or refuse to bring about the dissolution of the Defendant Company, this Court will entertain such proceedings as may be necessary for the proper dissolution of the Company.
V. This Court shall retain jurisdiction in this matter.
The court ordered matter continued to November 5, 2013 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514.
Court adjourned.
